input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML (`ItemsPage.xaml`)**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:MyApp.ViewModels"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.ItemsPage"
             x:DataType="vm:ItemsViewModel">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                    SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding ItemSelectedCommand}"
                    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
                    RemainingItemsThreshold="0">

        <!-- Pull-to-refresh requires wrapping in a RefreshView -->
        <CollectionView.Parent>
            <!-- See note below -->
        </CollectionView.Parent>

        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="vm:ItemModel">
                <VerticalStackLayout Padding="10">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="18" />
                    <Label Text="{Binding Description}" FontSize="14"
                           TextColor="Gray" />
                </VerticalStackLayout>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>
</ContentPage>
```

`CollectionView` doesn't natively support pull-to-refresh — wrap it in a `RefreshView`:

```xml
<RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
             IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">
    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                    SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding ItemSelectedCommand}">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="vm:ItemModel">
                <VerticalStackLayout Padding="10">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="18" />
                    <Label Text="{Binding Description}" FontSize="14"
                           TextColor="Gray" />
                </VerticalStackLayout>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>
</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel (`ItemsViewModel.cs`)**

```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<ItemModel> _items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ItemModel? _selectedItem;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    public ItemsViewModel(IApiService api)
    {
        _api = api;
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task Refresh()
    {
        try
        {
            var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
            Items = new ObservableCollection<ItemModel>(data);
        }
        finally
        {
            IsRefreshing = false;   // stops the spinner
        }
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task ItemSelected()
    {
        if (SelectedItem is null) return;

        await Shell.Current.GoToAsync(
            $"{nameof(DetailPage)}",
            new Dictionary<string, object> { ["Item"] = SelectedItem });

        SelectedItem = null;   // clear so the same item can be re-selected
    }
}
```

**Key binding summary:**

| XAML Property | Binds To | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `ItemsSource` | `Items` (ObservableCollection) | Data source |
| `SelectedItem` | `SelectedItem` | Tracks current selection |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` | `ItemSelectedCommand` | Fires on tap → navigates |
| `RefreshView.Command` | `RefreshCommand` | Fires on pull → reloads |
| `RefreshView.IsRefreshing` | `IsRefreshing` | Controls spinner visibility |

**Important details:**
- Set `SelectedItem = null` after navigation so the same row can be tapped again.
- Set `IsRefreshing = false` in a `finally` block so the spinner always stops.
- Uses **CommunityToolkit.Mvvm** source generators (`[ObservableProperty]`, `[RelayCommand]`).
- Register the route in `AppShell.xaml.cs`: `Routing.RegisterRoute(nameof(DetailPage), typeof(DetailPage));`
